freshness_policy.max_allowed_age_description: Portfolio action registers are refreshed from current management workflow state and governed portfolio-state inputs.

Bulk review campaign membership is generated from the source-backed candidate set supplied inline or through an immutable BulkReviewCampaignDefinition:v1 definition plus optional governance evidence for the requested campaign as-of date. Preview-readiness evaluates persisted definition lifecycle, requested as-of date, source-backed candidate eligibility, governance approval, expiry, and optional actor entitlement before new preview/create use. Workflow overview composes discovery, preview-readiness, lifecycle-event, launch-history, approval decisions, assignment actions, maker-checker controls, and optional launch-package posture without discovering the global portfolio universe or recalculating membership. The operating queue classifies persisted definitions into launch-ready, attention-required, and closed rows from existing discovery, preview-readiness, lifecycle, launch-history, and approval posture without adding order or OMS claims. The approval inbox classifies persisted definitions into approval-complete, approval-required, approval-incomplete, expiry-attention, entitlement-attention, and closed rows from existing governance evidence and readiness posture without adding trade-approval, order, or OMS claims. The workflow board composes the existing operating queue and approval inbox into actor-aware next-action rows for launch, approval-decision capture, approval evidence remediation, expiry refresh, entitlement review, or closed posture without mutating approval state. The assignment plan derives read-only actor routing, escalation tier, SLA posture, and reason codes from the workflow board without mutating assignment state or creating escalation tasks. Assignment actions provide append-only assignment, reassignment, escalation, de-escalation, and resolution posture evidence on active definitions without approval-state mutation, trade approval, order, client-contact, maker-checker, or OMS claims. Approval decisions provide append-only campaign approval posture evidence on active definitions without trade approval, order, client-contact, maker-checker, or OMS claims. Maker-checker controls provide append-only campaign control evidence on active definitions with actor separation required for completed reviews, without trade approval, order generation or routing, client contact, external workflow orchestration, or OMS claims. Launch packages provide a bounded preview/create request draft and required headers without recalculating campaign membership. Durable definition launch consumes the same readiness package, creates one wave only when ready, replays by deterministic launch idempotency, and records append-only launch history for wave lineage.

PM operating quality score runs are generated from explicit bank policy, bank approval and fairness-review evidence, current source-backed evidence supplied or referenced by the request, and optional lotus-core PortfolioManagerBookMembership:v1 scope materialization. Fairness analyses are generated from persisted score runs and source-defined operating segments. Review actions are immutable ledger rows over existing score-run or fairness-analysis evidence and preserve target content hashes without recalculating scores or recomputing fairness posture. Portfolio memory projects bounded score-run lineage only when persisted Core PM-book member evidence includes the requested portfolio.
